DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF DOREEN SINGH'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to defense 1 [failure to state a cause of action]. That is not an affirmative defense because an affirmative defense sets forth facts that diminish a cause of action or show that it is not cognizable even if the allegations in the complaint are true. See FPI Development, Inc. v. Al Nahashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 383; Goddard v. Fulton (1863) 21 Cal.430, 436. A failure to state a cause action is grounds for a demurrer or motion for judgment on the pleadings. The court sustains without leave to amend as to defenses 20 [no basis for class claim], 21 [Plaintiff lacks standing as class representative], 22 [facts do not warrant class certification], and 24 [improper UCL representative action] because those are reasons to oppose a motion for class certification. They are not defenses. Defense 32 [reservation of rights to raise new defenses] is not cognizable and the court sustains the demurrer to that defense without leave to amend. The court overrules as to defense 2 [statute of limitations]. The defense complies with CCP 458 [pleading statute of limitations defense] because defendant highlights the specific statute that allegedly time bars the claims. The court sustains with 20 days leave to amend as to the remaining defenses. They do not meet the pleading requirements of FPI Development. They are totally conclusory and defendant needs to allege ultimate facts to support the defenses. Lastly, there is nothing improper about defendant's request for attorney's fees and a subsequent motion regarding fees will resolve that issue. The Court signed the proposed order submitted by email. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT EAST WEST BANK'S Motion to Strike Portions of Plaintiff's Unverified Complaint is granted in part and denied in part. As to the references to and prayer for, the motion to strike is granted. (These portions are referred to in Defendant's Notice of Motion and Motion to Strike ("Motion"), on page 2, as items 1, 2, 3, and 6.) "'[D]amages are not available under section 17203.'" Cortez v. Purolator Air Filtration Products Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 163 at 173 (quoting Bank of the West v. Superior Court (1992) 2 Cal.4th 1254, 1266). Rather, remedies under Business and Professions Code 17200, et seq. are limited to equitable relief. The motion to strike is granted as to the demand for a jury trial (Motion, page 2, item 5.) There is no right to a jury trial where potential relief is solely equitable in nature. Wisden v. Superior Court (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 750, 755. Hodge v. Superior Court explicitly states that "There Is No Right To a Jury Trial For a Section 17200 Cause of Action," and that in determining the right to a jury trial, the court should consider the "gist of the UCL claim, not the gist of the Labor Code violation." (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 278, 283-84. The motion to strike is denied as to attorneys' fees and costs of suit. Plaintiff has pled each element of a claim under Code of Civil Procedure 1021.5 sufficiently to withstand a motion to strike and the determination of the merits of Plaintiff's arguments should be reserved to the trial court. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
